indicators.label: RBI Repo Rate
indicators.source: Reserve Bank of India — MPC Statement June 2026
indicators.interpretation: MPC voted unanimously to hold repo rate at 5.25% for the third consecutive meeting, maintaining a neutral stance. The previous rate was 5.50% before cumulative 125 bps of cuts since Feb 2025. With Brent now settled at ~$79.34/bbl on June 16 (vs RBI's elevated projection at the time of the June MPC), conditions for an August 2026 cut have further crystallised. Core CPI remains below 4%; August MPC (Aug 6-8) is a live cut meeting. US Fed holds at 3.5-3.75% (June 17 decision) providing external rate-environment stability.

indicators.label: CPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) — CPI Press Release May 2026
indicators.interpretation: CPI at 3.93% in May 2026 remains below the RBI's 4% midpoint target. Food inflation (CFPI) climbed to 4.78%. Brent has now settled at ~$79.34/bbl on June 16 (further from ~$83/bbl on June 15), accelerating crude disinflation. The June CPI print (due late June) should come in below 4% if food prices stay contained — a key condition for August MPC cut. Mumbai monsoon arrival pushed to ~June 25 adds upside risk to vegetable prices in late June.

indicators.label: WPI Inflation YoY (May 2026)
indicators.source: Ministry of Commerce & Industry — WPI Press Release May 2026 (new 2022-23 base series); Economic Times report
indicators.interpretation: WPI at 9.7% in May 2026 (new 2022-23 base series, covering 957 items vs 697 earlier) reflects war-era crude prices when Brent was above $100/bbl. Fuel & Power +30.3% YoY; crude petroleum +61.5%. Bank of Baroda estimates WPI will average 7-8% in FY27. The June WPI print (mid-July) is expected to moderate sharply as Brent has now fallen to ~$79/bbl — a ~37% decline from the conflict peak. India has also introduced a Producer Price Index (PPI) to eventually replace WPI, signalling a structural improvement in inflation measurement. May WPI is a lagging indicator of a crisis that has substantially resolved.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY26 (Provisional Actual)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Provisional Estimates of GDP for FY2025-26
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 real GDP grew 7.7%, the fastest pace in three years, beating consensus of 7.2-7.5%. Q4 FY26 clocked 7.8%. Manufacturing +10.7%; financial services and real estate +10.4%; trade, hotels, transport +11%. GFCF +10.8% in Q4 signals investment-led momentum. India Inc Q4 FY26 aggregate net profit grew 25.3% YoY — highest in nine quarters. This strong FY26 base provides solid platform for FY27.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY27 RBI Forecast
indicators.source: Reserve Bank of India — MPC Statement June 2026
indicators.interpretation: RBI's official FY27 GDP forecast was revised down to 6.6% (from 6.9%) at the June MPC, citing West Asia conflict energy risks. With crude now at ~$79/bbl, the actual FY27 outcome is likely to come in closer to 6.8-7.0% — making this forecast a conservative floor rather than a central scenario. Monarch Networth Capital (June 16) expects Nifty EPS at ₹1,251 for FY27 and ₹1,443 for FY28.

indicators.label: IIP Industrial Production Growth (April 2026)
indicators.source: MoSPI — Index of Industrial Production April 2026 (new base year 2022-23)
indicators.interpretation: Industrial output grew 4.9% YoY in April 2026 under the new IIP series (base year 2022-23). Manufacturing +6.2%; Capital goods +16% YoY — strongest sub-segment — reflecting public capex spend. Consumer durables +4.3% and consumer non-durables +2.8%, signalling uneven recovery with investment cycle leading consumption. Power Grid's TBCB win (June 16-17) and NHPC's 3,097 MW Etalin HEP MoA with Arunachal Pradesh (June 16) continue to signal public infrastructure investment momentum.

indicators.label: FII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD through June 16)
indicators.source: NSE India FII/DII Reports + Moneycontrol FII/DII Activity — NSE+BSE+MSEI combined provisional
indicators.interpretation: FIIs net sold ₹748 Cr on June 16 (NSE+BSE+MSEI combined, provisional). June MTD FII net: approx -₹46,978 Cr through June 16. YTD FII outflows: ~₹3.42 lakh crore (as of June 16). FII equity flows oscillated near zero June 15-16 (net +₹200 Cr on June 15, -₹748 Cr on June 16) — far less aggressive than May's ₹8,000-9,000 crore/day selling. FII bond buying accelerated to $1.84 billion in June (16-month high), suggesting the macro case for India is being re-rated in fixed income first. Equity re-entry catalyst: India-US BTA signing (talks June 23-24, expected mid-July).

indicators.label: DII Net Flows (June 2026 MTD through June 16)
indicators.source: NSE India FII/DII Reports + Moneycontrol FII/DII Activity — NSE+BSE+MSEI combined provisional
indicators.interpretation: DIIs effectively flat on June 16 (net bought ~₹6 lakh — near-historic low DII net activity — as markets rose 0.57% on retail/proprietary buying). June MTD DII net: +₹61,137 Cr through June 15. YTD DII purchases: +₹4.4 lakh crore as of June 16, significantly exceeding FII outflows of ₹3.42 lakh crore YTD. Monthly SIP flows remain structurally firm at ₹31,115 Cr/month (April). DII institutional buying of +₹4.4 lakh crore YTD is the structural price-setter and demand floor.

indicators.label: USD/INR Exchange Rate
indicators.source: Economic Times / The Hindu Business Line — USD/INR June 16 2026 closing
indicators.interpretation: The rupee closed at 94.56 per dollar on June 16 — up 15 paise from June 15's close of 94.71 — its third consecutive session of appreciation as Brent crude fell to ~$79.34/bbl and US-Iran peace deal details firmed. The rupee has strengthened from ~96.8 in May (war peak) by over 224 paise in three weeks. Pre-market June 17: ~94.5-94.7 per x-rates.com data (94.497 as of June 17 02:27 UTC). Continued rupee strength reduces inflation import pressure and India's current account deficit. Mild headwind for IT and pharma dollar-denominated exporters but substantially positive for macro stability.

indicators.label: Brent Crude Oil (USD/bbl)
indicators.source: MarketScreener / OilPriceAPI / Straits Times — Brent crude June 16-17 2026 settlement
indicators.interpretation: Brent crude settled at ~$79.34/bbl on June 16 (The Star: 'fell $4.21 or 5.1% to settle at $78.96'; CNBC June 17: 'rose 0.6% to $79.43 as investors weighed durability of deal'; OilPriceAPI June 17: $79.34-79.48). Brent is now below $80 for the first time since March — back at pre-US-Iran conflict levels. From the $124/bbl conflict peak, Brent has fallen ~36% in two trading sessions. Goldman Sachs cut Brent forecast to average $80/bbl Q4 2026 (from $90); Morgan Stanley also cut forecast. At $79/bbl, India's annual crude import bill vs war-peak is ~$65-70 billion lower — the single most powerful macro tailwind for India's CAD, CPI, and monetary policy transmission. Crude below $80 makes August RBI rate cut the near-certain base case.

indicators.label: India 10Y G-Sec Yield
indicators.source: Economic Times / The Hindu Business Line — India 10Y G-sec June 16 2026 settlement
indicators.interpretation: India's 10-year benchmark G-sec yield (6.94% GS 2036) settled at 6.8651% on June 16, down from 6.8704% on June 15 and 7.02% two weeks ago — at its lowest since March 24. The yield traded in a narrow 6.86-6.87% range as markets absorbed early crude disinflation relief against sparse US-Iran deal details. Pre-market June 17: The Hindu Business Line guide for the session is 6.85-6.87% range as Brent has settled further at ~$79.34/bbl. FIIs invested $1.84 billion (₹17,000 crore) in Indian bonds in June — highest in 16 months. Falling bond yields reduce the cost of capital economy-wide and structurally support NBFC, realty, and capital goods re-rating. Bank of Maharashtra's MCLR hike (effective June 17) signals some banks are still managing deposit cost pressures independently of the repo rate.

indicators.label: Fiscal Deficit FY26 (Provisional Actuals)
indicators.source: Moneycontrol — India FY26 fiscal deficit at 4.4%, in line with govt estimates
indicators.interpretation: India's FY26 fiscal deficit came in at 4.4% of GDP (₹15.19 lakh crore), meeting the government's revised target. For FY27, the April deficit touched 21.4% of the annual target — seasonal and expected. The government's FY27 fiscal target remains 4.4% of GDP. GIC Re OFS (June 16-17) raising ₹3,088 crore (subscribed 3.72x on Day 1, greenshoe exercised) demonstrates robust demand for divestment assets and adds to FY27 revenue receipts. Sustained fiscal discipline is positive for sovereign rating trajectory and bond market confidence.

indicators.label: GST Collections (May 2026)
indicators.source: Economic Times — GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 lakh crore in May
indicators.interpretation: Gross GST in May 2026: ₹1.94 lakh crore, up 3.2% YoY. Moderation from April's record ₹2.42 lakh crore is seasonal. Import-linked GST surged 19.7% (war-era crude-driven) while domestic revenue fell 2.3% YoY — suggesting underlying domestic consumption softness. April-May combined GST: ₹4.37 lakh crore (+6.2% YoY). June GST (released July 1) should moderate further on lower crude import values as Brent has now fallen to ~$79/bbl.

indicators.label: Southwest Monsoon 2026 — Progress & IMD Forecast
indicators.value: Monsoon advance into Telangana/Odisha/Jharkhand/Bihar/Chhattisgarh next 4-5 days; Mumbai arrival delayed to ~June 25; Western/Central India subdued; cumulative deficit persists from break period June 16-21
indicators.prev: 28% below normal cumulative rainfall as of June 15; season forecast 90% of LPA
indicators.interpretation: IMD (June 17) forecasts monsoon advance into Telangana, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar, and parts of Chhattisgarh over the next 4-5 days — a marginal positive progression. However, Western India (Maharashtra, Gujarat, Rajasthan) and Central India remain subdued; Mumbai monsoon onset has been pushed back to ~June 25, two weeks past the city's normal June 11 arrival. The break period of June 16-21 deepens the early-season rainfall deficit. Monsoon revival expected in the June 22-28 period with cross-equatorial winds strengthening. A fuller recovery target: June 29-July 5 per weather models. Key risk: continued deficiency would pressure food inflation, rural FMCG/two-wheeler volumes, agri-NBFC credit quality, and delay Kharif crop sowing in Central/Northwest India through Q2 FY27.

indicators.label: FII/FPI Net Cash (provisional)
indicators.interpretation: Net institutional foreign flow can influence near-term index direction and liquidity.

indicators.label: DII Net Cash (provisional)
indicators.interpretation: Domestic flows often stabilize drawdowns; strong buys support breadth.

indicators.label: India VIX
indicators.interpretation: Lower implied volatility typically supports risk-on positioning; spikes can precede risk-off moves.

marketState.headline: Domestic institutional buying remains supportive; volatility stays low.

boosting.detail: Provisional domestic buying is strong versus recent sessions.

lagging.detail: FII participation is only marginally positive on provisional basis.

marketState.narration: With DIIs net buyers and implied volatility low, the tape stays constructive, but confidence is capped until follow-through above key resistance levels is visible in price action.
marketState.method: Weighted read of institutional flows (FII/DII provisional) and volatility (India VIX). Other macro prints marked DNA unless refreshed from official sources.
